_meta.description: Static analysis rule set used by the security_engineer agent's SAST pass over src/. Each rule is applied as a regex match against files matching filePattern. Findings are emitted with the rule's severity and category so downstream triage can group them consistently.

rules.title: eval() usage
rules.description: Use of eval() enables arbitrary code execution when any operand is attacker-influenced. Replace with structured parsing or a safe alternative.

rules.title: dangerouslySetInnerHTML without explicit sanitizer
rules.description: Any usage of dangerouslySetInnerHTML is flagged for manual review. Ensure the HTML has been sanitized with a trusted library (e.g. DOMPurify) before rendering.

rules.title: Hardcoded secret in source
rules.description: A string literal of at least 24 characters is assigned to a variable whose name looks like a credential (key, secret, token, password, apiKey, accessKey). Confirm the value is not a real secret and move it to environment configuration if it is.

rules.title: API route handler missing auth guard
rules.description: A route handler under src/app/api/**/route.ts exports an HTTP method function that does not appear to call requireAuth, requireAdmin, requireSession, getSession, or a similar guard. Flagged as review; many public endpoints legitimately skip auth.

rules.title: State-changing handler lacking CSRF / origin check
rules.description: A POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH route handler body does not reference a CSRF token validator or origin/referrer check. Heuristic; Next.js same-origin forms and pure JSON APIs with SameSite cookies may be acceptable without explicit CSRF tokens — confirm case-by-case.

rules.title: Raw NoSQL injection pattern
rules.description: User-controlled input is concatenated into a MongoDB $where clause or flows directly into a Mongo query operator ($ne, $gt, $regex, etc.) without sanitization. Use typed query builders and coerce user input to the expected primitive type.
